notes: Gold consumable rules for career_outcomes table. institution_control is known 100% NULL (Silver blocking issue); NOT NULL constraint relaxed to tracked completeness rule (P2). DTE tier plurality corrected from spec's 'Moderate' to actual 'Low' per EDA finding. Golden dataset rule (CONS-GOLDEN-DATASET) is DEFERRED pending golden dataset creation by @primary-agent. Concept normalization patterns (CONS-COLLISION-RESOLVED, CONS-COVERAGE-FLOOR) do not apply — single-source spec with no concept normalization.

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: unitid x cipcode x credential_level
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, cipcode, credential_level, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_outcomes GROUP BY unitid, cipcode, credential_level HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per unitid x cipcode x credential_level. Any duplicate indicates a promote or transformer failure. Maps to patterns CONS-GRAIN-UNIQUE and ADV-GRAIN-UNIQUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Silver has 0 duplicates. Hard constraint.' Logical model: 'Zero duplicates on natural key. Enforced at promote time.' 69,947 rows in Silver with 0 grain violations. Threshold: 100% unique.

rules.name: Record ID uniqueness
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_outcomes GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: Surrogate key record_id (computed via compute_grain_id with prefix 'co') must be non-null and unique. Duplicate or null record_id indicates a grain hash collision or derivation bug.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'Record ID uniqueness — Zero duplicates on record_id.' Deterministic SHA-256 hash of natural key should produce 0 collisions for 69,947 rows. Silver equivalent (SLV-CS-031) has 0 violations.

rules.name: Row count within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) >= 59455 AND COUNT(*) <= 80439 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: Gold table row count must be within +/- 15% of Silver source (69,947). Range: 59,455 to 80,439. All Silver rows are carried forward with no filtering.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Row count: 59,455 - 80,439 (+/- 15% of 69,947). All rows carried forward, no filtering.' Logical model: 'Expected cardinality: 69,947 rows (all Silver base rows carried forward).' Spec: 'Output Row Count: ~69,947 (all rows carried forward).'

rules.name: Percentile band ordering: earnings_1yr p25 <= p75
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE earnings_1yr_p25 IS NOT NULL AND earnings_1yr_p75 IS NOT NULL AND earnings_1yr_p25 > earnings_1yr_p75
rules.description: Within every CIP family, the 25th percentile of 1yr earnings must not exceed the 75th percentile. This is a mathematical invariant of PERCENTILE_CONT.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'P25 <= P75 invariant: 0 violations. This invariant will always hold by mathematical definition of PERCENTILE_CONT.' Logical model hard constraint: 'earnings_1yr_p25 <= earnings_1yr_p75.' 0 violations in simulation across all 38 valid CIP families.

rules.name: Percentile band ordering: earnings_2yr p25 <= p75
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE earnings_2yr_p25 IS NOT NULL AND earnings_2yr_p75 IS NOT NULL AND earnings_2yr_p25 > earnings_2yr_p75
rules.description: Within every CIP family, the 25th percentile of 2yr earnings must not exceed the 75th percentile.
rules.rationale: Same mathematical invariant as GLD-CO-004. EDA confirms 0 violations for all band pairs. Logical model hard constraint.

rules.name: Percentile band ordering: debt p25 <= p75
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE debt_p25 IS NOT NULL AND debt_p75 IS NOT NULL AND debt_p25 > debt_p75
rules.description: Within every CIP family, the 25th percentile of debt must not exceed the 75th percentile.
rules.rationale: Same mathematical invariant as GLD-CO-004/005. EDA confirms 0 violations. Logical model hard constraint.

rules.name: confidence_tier not null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE confidence_tier IS NULL
rules.description: Every row must have a confidence_tier assigned. This is the only derived field that is NOT NULL regardless of input data availability.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'confidence_tier NOT NULL — 0 nulls. Derived field, always computed.' Logical model: 'confidence_tier IS NOT NULL for every row.' All 69,947 rows receive a tier classification.

rules.name: confidence_tier value set
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE confidence_tier NOT IN ('high', 'medium', 'low', 'insufficient')
rules.description: confidence_tier must be one of exactly 4 values: 'high', 'medium', 'low', 'insufficient'. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'confidence_tier value set: IN (high, medium, low, insufficient). Exactly 4 values.' Logical model derivation defines exactly 4 mutually exclusive tiers. 69,947 rows, 0 values outside set.

rules.name: has_earnings accuracy
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE has_earnings != (earnings_1yr_median IS NOT NULL OR earnings_2yr_median IS NOT NULL)
rules.description: has_earnings must exactly equal (earnings_1yr_median IS NOT NULL OR earnings_2yr_median IS NOT NULL). Any mismatch indicates a derivation bug.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'has_earnings accuracy: 100% match. Derived from exact null check.' Logical model: 'has_earnings = (earnings_1yr_median IS NOT NULL OR earnings_2yr_median IS NOT NULL).' Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.

rules.name: has_debt accuracy
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE has_debt != (debt_median IS NOT NULL)
rules.description: has_debt must exactly equal (debt_median IS NOT NULL). Any mismatch indicates a derivation bug.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'has_debt accuracy: 100% match. Derived from exact null check.' Logical model: 'has_debt = (debt_median IS NOT NULL).' Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.

rules.name: outcome_completeness value set
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE outcome_completeness IS NULL OR ROUND(outcome_completeness, 2) NOT IN (0.0, 0.33, 0.67, 1.0)
rules.description: outcome_completeness must be exactly one of {0.0, 0.33, 0.67, 1.0} representing 0/3, 1/3, 2/3, or 3/3 core outcome fields populated. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'outcome_completeness value set: IN (0.0, 0.33, 0.67, 1.0). Exactly 4 values. No intermediate values are possible.' Logical model: derivation is count of non-null among 3 fields divided by 3.0. 69,947 rows, 0 values outside set.

rules.name: Null propagation: debt_to_earnings null when input null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE (debt_median IS NULL OR earnings_1yr_median IS NULL) AND debt_to_earnings_annual IS NOT NULL
rules.description: debt_to_earnings_annual must be null whenever either debt_median or earnings_1yr_median is null. Non-null ratio with null inputs indicates a derivation bug.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Null propagation: DTE null when input null — 100% consistency. debt_to_earnings_annual is null exactly when debt_median IS NULL OR earnings_1yr_median IS NULL.' Logical model: 'Null if either input is null.' Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.

rules.name: Null propagation: debt_to_earnings NOT null when inputs present
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE debt_median IS NOT NULL AND earnings_1yr_median IS NOT NULL AND debt_to_earnings_annual IS NULL
rules.description: debt_to_earnings_annual must be computed (non-null) whenever both inputs are present. A null ratio with non-null inputs indicates a derivation bug.
rules.rationale: EDA report shows 21,763 rows where both inputs are non-null and all produce a valid ratio (range 0.050-5.328). Logical model derivation: 'debt_median / earnings_1yr_median'. 0 missing computations expected.

rules.name: Null propagation: earnings_growth_rate null when input null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE (earnings_1yr_median IS NULL OR earnings_2yr_median IS NULL) AND earnings_growth_rate IS NOT NULL
rules.description: earnings_growth_rate must be null whenever either earnings_1yr_median or earnings_2yr_median is null.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'Null if either input is null.' Derivation: (earnings_2yr_median - earnings_1yr_median) / earnings_1yr_median. Null propagation is a hard constraint on all derived financial metrics.

rules.name: Null propagation: cip_family_earnings_rank null when earnings null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE earnings_1yr_median IS NULL AND cip_family_earnings_rank IS NOT NULL
rules.description: cip_family_earnings_rank must be null when earnings_1yr_median is null (excluded from PERCENT_RANK window).
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'Null if earnings_1yr_median is null (excluded from window).' EDA: rank population is 25,196 rows (same as non-null earnings_1yr_median count).

rules.name: Null propagation: program_value_index null when input null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE (earnings_1yr_median IS NULL OR debt_median IS NULL) AND program_value_index IS NOT NULL
rules.description: program_value_index must be null whenever either earnings_1yr_median or debt_median is null.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'Null if either input is null.' Derivation: earnings_1yr_median / debt_median. Same null propagation invariant as debt_to_earnings_annual.

rules.name: Null propagation: debt_to_earnings_tier null when ratio null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE (debt_to_earnings_annual IS NULL AND debt_to_earnings_tier IS NOT NULL) OR (debt_to_earnings_annual IS NOT NULL AND debt_to_earnings_tier IS NULL)
rules.description: debt_to_earnings_tier must be null exactly when debt_to_earnings_annual is null, and non-null otherwise.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'Null if ratio is null.' The tier is a direct bucketing of the ratio — they must share the same null pattern. EDA: 21,763 non-null ratios, 48,184 nulls.

rules.name: debt_to_earnings_tier value set
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE debt_to_earnings_tier IS NOT NULL AND debt_to_earnings_tier NOT IN ('Low', 'Moderate', 'High', 'Very High')
rules.description: When non-null, debt_to_earnings_tier must be one of exactly 4 values. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: Logical model derivation defines exactly 4 tiers: Low (<0.75), Moderate (0.75-1.5), High (1.5-2.5), Very High (>=2.5). EDA: all 21,763 non-null ratios map to one of these tiers.

rules.name: debt_to_earnings_annual range (where non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE debt_to_earnings_annual IS NOT NULL AND (debt_to_earnings_annual < 0.01 OR debt_to_earnings_annual > 10.0)
rules.description: When non-null, debt_to_earnings_annual must be between 0.01 and 10.0. Values outside this range indicate data quality issues in source earnings or debt fields.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Outside 0.01-10.0: 0 rows (0%). The spec's soft constraint range has zero violations.' Actual observed range: 0.050-5.328. The 0.01-10.0 bound is conservative with zero violations expected.

rules.name: earnings_growth_rate range (where non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE earnings_growth_rate IS NOT NULL AND (earnings_growth_rate < -0.5 OR earnings_growth_rate > 2.0)) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE earnings_growth_rate IS NOT NULL), 0) <= 0.5 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: No more than 0.5% of non-null earnings_growth_rate values may fall outside the range -0.5 to 2.0. Known outliers are expected from cross-cohort volatility.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '15 of 22,146 (0.068%) outside range -0.5 to 2.0. Set soft constraint; allow up to 0.5% outside range.' 13 below -0.5 (cross-cohort volatility), 2 above 2.0 (extreme cohort difference). These are legitimate edge cases, not data errors.

rules.name: cip_family_earnings_rank range (where non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE cip_family_earnings_rank IS NOT NULL AND (cip_family_earnings_rank < 0.0 OR cip_family_earnings_rank > 1.0)
rules.description: PERCENT_RANK always produces values in [0.0, 1.0]. Any value outside this range indicates a computation error.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'cip_family_earnings_rank range 0.0-1.0. Mathematical range of PERCENT_RANK. Zero violations.' Population: 25,196 rows. Mean=0.4997, confirming near-uniform distribution.

rules.name: DTE tier distribution: Low is plurality (of non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE debt_to_earnings_tier = 'Low') >= (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE debt_to_earnings_tier = 'Moderate') THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: The 'Low' DTE tier must be the plurality (most frequent non-null tier). The spec incorrectly expected 'Moderate' to be plurality; EDA corrected this.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'CORRECTION FOR SPEC: The spec states Moderate is plurality. Actual data shows Low is plurality at 69.23%.' Distribution: Low=69.23%, Moderate=29.96%, High=0.78%, Very High=0.02%. Low exceeds Moderate by a wide margin.

rules.name: DTE tier distribution: Low rate within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE debt_to_earnings_tier = 'Low') / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE debt_to_earnings_tier IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) BETWEEN 60.0 AND 80.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: The 'Low' DTE tier should comprise 60-80% of non-null tier assignments.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'DTE tier distribution: Low rate (of non-null) 60-80%. Current: 69.23%.' Centered in the expected range with margin for source data variation.

rules.name: DTE tier distribution: High + Very High rate capped
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE debt_to_earnings_tier IN ('High', 'Very High')) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE debt_to_earnings_tier IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) <= 3.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: The combined 'High' and 'Very High' DTE tiers should not exceed 3% of non-null assignments.
rules.rationale: EDA report: High=0.78%, Very High=0.02%, combined=0.80%. EDA threshold recommendations: High 0-3%, Very High 0-0.5%. 3% cap provides headroom.

rules.name: Confidence tier distribution: insufficient rate within range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE confidence_tier = 'insufficient') / COUNT(*), 1) BETWEEN 45.0 AND 60.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: The 'insufficient' confidence tier should comprise 45-60% of all rows.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Confidence tier: insufficient rate 45-60%. Current: 52.75%.' Spec estimate was 50-55%, confirmed by EDA. Range widened slightly for source data variation.

rules.name: Confidence tier distribution: high rate within range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE confidence_tier = 'high') / COUNT(*), 1) BETWEEN 15.0 AND 30.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: The 'high' confidence tier should comprise 15-30% of all rows.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Confidence tier: high rate 15-30%. Current: 21.80%.' This tier represents large-cohort programs with complete outcome data.

rules.name: Negative earnings_growth_rate within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE earnings_growth_rate IS NOT NULL AND earnings_growth_rate < 0) / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE earnings_growth_rate IS NOT NULL), 0), 1) BETWEEN 35.0 AND 55.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: 35-55% of non-null earnings growth rates should be negative. This is expected due to cross-cohort measurement (not longitudinal tracking).
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Negative growth rate: 35-55% of non-null. Current: 44.2%.' Domain context: '2yr earnings < 1yr earnings (44.2%) — different cohort measurement windows, not longitudinal tracking.'

rules.name: Percentile band null pattern: 1yr earnings bands null for small CIP families
rules.sql: SELECT co.cip_family, COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes co WHERE co.earnings_1yr_p25 IS NOT NULL AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.career_outcomes co2 WHERE co2.cip_family = co.cip_family AND co2.earnings_1yr_median IS NOT NULL) < 3 GROUP BY co.cip_family
rules.description: CIP families with fewer than 3 non-null earnings_1yr_median values must have null percentile bands. Non-null bands for these families indicate a transformer bug.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '7 CIP families will get null 1yr earnings percentile bands (< 3 non-null values): CIP 28, 32, 33, 34, 35, 48, 53. Affects 36 rows (0.05%).' Logical model: 'Null if fewer than 3 non-null values in the CIP family for this field.'

rules.name: Confidence tier derivation: insufficient iff no outcome data
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE (confidence_tier = 'insufficient' AND (has_earnings = true OR has_debt = true)) OR (confidence_tier != 'insufficient' AND has_earnings = false AND has_debt = false)
rules.description: confidence_tier = 'insufficient' if and only if has_earnings = false AND has_debt = false. This is the logical model's key invariant linking confidence tier to outcome data availability.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'insufficient: has_earnings = False AND has_debt = False.' EDA: 36,894 rows are insufficient (52.75%), all matching the no-outcome-data pattern. The converse must also hold: any row with no outcome data must be insufficient.

rules.name: Confidence tier derivation: high requires large cohort + complete data
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE confidence_tier = 'high' AND NOT (small_cohort_flag = false AND has_earnings = true AND has_debt = true)
rules.description: confidence_tier = 'high' requires small_cohort_flag = false AND has_earnings = true AND has_debt = true. Any row assigned 'high' without meeting all three conditions is a derivation error.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'high: small_cohort_flag = False AND has_earnings = True AND has_debt = True.' EDA: 15,245 rows are high (21.80%), matching cross-tabulation of Flag=False + both earnings and debt present.

rules.name: outcome_completeness consistency with underlying fields
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE ROUND(outcome_completeness, 2) != ROUND((CASE WHEN earnings_1yr_median IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN earnings_2yr_median IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN debt_median IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / 3.0, 2)
rules.description: outcome_completeness must exactly match the count of non-null core outcome fields divided by 3. Validates the derivation logic.
rules.rationale: Logical model derivation: '(CASE WHEN earnings_1yr IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END + ... + CASE WHEN debt IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / 3.0.' EDA: value distribution matches exactly: 0.0=52.75%, 0.33=10.11%, 0.67=9.05%, 1.0=28.09%. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.

rules.name: NOT NULL fields completeness: required identity fields
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE record_id IS NULL OR unitid IS NULL OR institution_name IS NULL OR cipcode IS NULL OR program_name IS NULL OR cip_family IS NULL OR cip_family_name IS NULL OR credential_level IS NULL OR small_cohort_flag IS NULL OR source_load_date IS NULL OR promoted_at IS NULL
rules.description: All NOT NULL fields per the logical model must be populated. Covers: record_id, unitid, institution_name, cipcode, program_name, cip_family, cip_family_name, credential_level, small_cohort_flag, source_load_date, promoted_at.
rules.rationale: Logical model defines 14 NOT NULL attributes. Silver DQ rules (SLV-CS-005 through SLV-CS-015) confirm 0 nulls for all carried-forward NOT NULL fields. institution_control excluded due to known 100% NULL blocking issue.

rules.name: NOT NULL fields completeness: derived quality fields
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE confidence_tier IS NULL OR has_earnings IS NULL OR has_debt IS NULL OR outcome_completeness IS NULL
rules.description: All derived data quality fields must be populated for every row: confidence_tier, has_earnings, has_debt, outcome_completeness.
rules.rationale: Logical model: all four data confidence attributes are NOT NULL. They are derived from conditional logic that always produces a value (no null propagation). EDA confirms: confidence_tier 0% null, has_earnings 0% null, has_debt 0% null, outcome_completeness 0% null.

rules.name: Distinct institution count within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) >= 2200 AND COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) <= 3000 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: Gold table should maintain the same institution coverage as Silver: 2,200-3,000 distinct institutions. Maps to ADV-ENTITY-COVERAGE pattern.
rules.rationale: Silver DQ rule SLV-CS-029: '2,559 distinct institutions.' Silver EDA established range 2,200-3,000. Gold carries forward all Silver rows, so entity coverage should be identical.

rules.name: Distinct CIP families within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT cip_family) >= 40 AND COUNT(DISTINCT cip_family) <= 50 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: Gold table should contain 40-50 distinct CIP families, matching Silver.
rules.rationale: Silver DQ rule SLV-CS-032: '45 distinct CIP families.' Gold carries forward all rows. Maps to ADV-ENTITY-COVERAGE pattern.

rules.name: credential_level must equal 3
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE credential_level != 3
rules.description: MVP is filtered to bachelor's degrees only (credential_level = 3). Any other value indicates a filter failure upstream.
rules.rationale: Silver DQ rule SLV-CS-025: '0 violations.' Domain context: 'CREDLEV must equal 3.' Logical model: 'credential_level = 3 in MVP scope.' Carried from Silver verbatim.

rules.name: program_value_index is mathematical inverse of DTE
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE debt_to_earnings_annual IS NOT NULL AND program_value_index IS NOT NULL AND ABS(program_value_index - (1.0 / debt_to_earnings_annual)) > 0.001
rules.description: program_value_index (earnings/debt) must be the mathematical inverse of debt_to_earnings_annual (debt/earnings), within floating point tolerance.
rules.rationale: Logical model: 'program_value_index = earnings_1yr_median / debt_median' and 'debt_to_earnings_annual = debt_median / earnings_1yr_median.' EDA confirms: min PVI = 0.188 corresponds to max DTE = 5.328, and max PVI = 19.865 corresponds to min DTE = 0.050. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.

rules.name: DTE tier boundaries match ratio values
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE debt_to_earnings_tier IS NOT NULL AND NOT ((debt_to_earnings_tier = 'Low' AND debt_to_earnings_annual < 0.75) OR (debt_to_earnings_tier = 'Moderate' AND debt_to_earnings_annual >= 0.75 AND debt_to_earnings_annual < 1.5) OR (debt_to_earnings_tier = 'High' AND debt_to_earnings_annual >= 1.5 AND debt_to_earnings_annual < 2.5) OR (debt_to_earnings_tier = 'Very High' AND debt_to_earnings_annual >= 2.5))
rules.description: The debt_to_earnings_tier must match the exact bucketing boundaries defined in the logical model. Every non-null tier must correspond to the correct ratio range.
rules.rationale: Logical model derivation: Low <0.75, Moderate 0.75-1.5, High 1.5-2.5, Very High >=2.5. EDA confirmed tier distribution matches these boundaries exactly. This rule detects tier misassignment bugs.

rules.name: institution_control completeness tracking
rules.sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN institution_control IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS null_pct FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: Tracks institution_control null rate. Currently 100% NULL due to Bronze re-ingestion blocker. This is a tracking rule, not a blocker. Target: 0% once CONTROL field is added to Bronze parquet.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'institution_control = NULL: 69,947 (100%). BLOCKING — Bronze re-ingestion needed.' Logical model lists as NOT NULL, but EDA Finding #7 identifies this as a known blocking issue. Rule tracks progress toward resolution.

rules.name: CIP families with null 1yr bands count within range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT cip_family) <= 10 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE earnings_1yr_p25 IS NULL AND earnings_1yr_median IS NOT NULL
rules.description: The number of CIP families where a program has earnings data but null percentile bands should not exceed 10. This catches unexpected expansion of the null-band population.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'CIP families with null bands count <= 10. Currently 7-8 depending on field.' 7 families for 1yr earnings bands. Threshold of 10 provides headroom for source data variation.

rules.name: Mean debt-to-earnings ratio within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN AVG(debt_to_earnings_annual) BETWEEN 0.50 AND 0.80 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE debt_to_earnings_annual IS NOT NULL
rules.description: The mean DTE ratio across all non-null values should be between 0.50 and 0.80. Monitors for distribution drift. Maps to ADV-VALUE-RANGE / ADV-DISTRIBUTION-VARIANCE patterns.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Mean debt-to-earnings ratio: 0.50-0.80. Current: 0.647.' Median=0.616, Std Dev=0.274. Range is centered on the observed mean with room for data variation.

rules.name: Mean earnings growth rate within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN AVG(earnings_growth_rate) BETWEEN -0.05 AND 0.10 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE earnings_growth_rate IS NOT NULL
rules.description: The mean earnings growth rate across all non-null values should be between -0.05 and +0.10. Monitors for distribution drift.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Mean earnings growth rate: -0.05 to +0.10. Current: +0.031.' Distribution is approximately normal, centered slightly positive. Range provides headroom for cohort variation.

rules.name: CSI enrichment preserves row count exactly
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 69947 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: Post-enrichment row count of consumable.career_outcomes must equal the pre-enrichment count exactly (69,947). The LEFT JOIN on institution attributes must not drop, duplicate, or fan-out any base rows.
rules.rationale: EDA `docs/sessions/eda-gold-career-outcomes-csi-enrichment.md` verified consumable.career_outcomes = 69,947 rows. Spec `docs/specs/raw-ingest-college-scorecard-institution.md` §Zone 3 GLD-CSI-001 declares row count invariance under LEFT JOIN enrichment. Any drift signals a join-cardinality bug (duplicate unitid in Silver institution table or dropped rows from INNER JOIN regression).

rules.name: Net price cannot exceed cost of attendance
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE net_price_annual IS NOT NULL AND cost_of_attendance_annual IS NOT NULL AND net_price_annual > cost_of_attendance_annual
rules.description: For rows where both fields are non-null, net_price_annual (cost after aid) must be less than or equal to cost_of_attendance_annual (sticker price). Net price exceeding sticker is a mathematical impossibility.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 3 GLD-CSI-002 hard invariant. EDA confirmed Silver `silver_base.college_scorecard_institution` enforces this via SLV-CSI consistency rule (0 violations). Gold carries these fields forward unchanged; any violation here indicates a derivation or join corruption bug. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.

rules.name: 4-year net price equals annual times 4
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE net_price_4yr IS NOT NULL AND net_price_annual IS NOT NULL AND ABS(net_price_4yr - net_price_annual * 4) > 1
rules.description: Derived field net_price_4yr must equal net_price_annual * 4 within a $1 rounding tolerance for rows where both are non-null. Validates the Zone 3 enrichment arithmetic.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 3 GLD-CSI-003 hard invariant. Spec §Enrichment Mode defines net_price_4yr = net_price_annual * 4 as a pure derivation. $1 tolerance accommodates integer rounding during transform. EDA confirmed both source fields co-null at identical 4.55% rate, so the derivation pairs cleanly. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.

rules.name: Net price lower bound respects BT-111 legitimate negatives
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE net_price_annual IS NOT NULL AND net_price_annual < -10000
rules.description: When non-null, net_price_annual must be >= -$10,000. Negative values above this floor are legitimate per business term BT-111 (aid packages exceeding cost of attendance); values below indicate source corruption.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 3 GLD-CSI-004 floor threshold. Business glossary BT-111 documents that Scorecard NPT4_PUB/NPT4_PRIV can be legitimately negative when grant aid exceeds sticker price at need-blind institutions. EDA confirmed Silver enforces identical -$10k floor with 0 violations; Gold inherits the constraint.

rules.name: net_price_annual completeness >= 90%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN net_price_annual IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) >= 90.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: At least 90% of rows in consumable.career_outcomes must have a non-null net_price_annual after CSI enrichment. Observed coverage is 95.45%; the 90% floor leaves a ~5 percentage point drift buffer.
rules.rationale: EDA `docs/sessions/eda-gold-career-outcomes-csi-enrichment.md`: post-join null rate for net_price_annual is 4.55% (coverage 95.45%) across 69,947 rows. Spec's pre-EDA placeholder (~73%) was based on an overestimate of unmatched UNITIDs (~1,131); actual unmatched = 207, so the true coverage is much higher. Threshold calibrated to 90% (5.45pp drift buffer) per data-analyst recommendation.

rules.name: cost_of_attendance_annual completeness >= 90%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN cost_of_attendance_annual IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) >= 90.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: At least 90% of rows must have a non-null cost_of_attendance_annual after CSI enrichment. Observed coverage is 95.45%; the 90% floor leaves a ~5 percentage point drift buffer.
rules.rationale: EDA `docs/sessions/eda-gold-career-outcomes-csi-enrichment.md`: post-join null rate for cost_of_attendance_annual is 4.55% (coverage 95.45%), co-null with net_price_annual as expected (institutions reporting one field almost always report the other). Spec's pre-EDA placeholder (~73%) was based on inflated unmatched-UNITID estimate. Threshold calibrated to 90% per data-analyst recommendation.

rules.name: institution_control completeness >= 95%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN institution_control IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) >= 95.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes
rules.description: At least 95% of rows must have a non-null institution_control after CSI enrichment. Observed coverage is 97.42%; the 95% floor leaves a ~2.4 percentage point drift buffer. Supersedes the legacy GLD-CO-039 tracking rule once CSI enrichment ships.
rules.rationale: EDA `docs/sessions/eda-gold-career-outcomes-csi-enrichment.md`: post-join null rate for institution_control is 2.58% (coverage 97.42%) — higher coverage than the cost fields because Scorecard reports CONTROL even for small institutions that lack full net-price reporting. Threshold tightened to 95% per data-analyst recommendation (2.42pp drift buffer). This rule resolves the Silver institution_control blocker that GLD-CO-039 tracks.

rules.name: Unmatched UNITID count <= 300
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT unitid) <= 300 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE institution_control IS NULL AND net_price_annual IS NULL AND cost_of_attendance_annual IS NULL
rules.description: The count of distinct UNITIDs that failed to match the Silver institution table (rows where all three enriched attributes are NULL) must not exceed 300. Observed unmatched = 207; threshold provides a 93-unit drift buffer.
rules.rationale: EDA `docs/sessions/eda-gold-career-outcomes-csi-enrichment.md`: 2,559 distinct UNITIDs in consumable.career_outcomes; 2,352 matched the Silver institution table (91.9%); 207 unmatched. Spec §Enrichment Mode note 5 predicted ~1,131 unmatched based on a miscalculated universe — actual is ~5.5x smaller. Threshold of 300 per data-analyst recommendation gives a 93-UNITID drift buffer (~45% headroom over observed) for Scorecard vintage churn. Maps to ADV-ENTITY-COVERAGE pattern.

rules.name: institution_control value set
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE institution_control IS NOT NULL AND institution_control NOT IN ('Public', 'Private nonprofit', 'Private for-profit')
rules.description: When non-null, institution_control must be one of exactly three canonical values: 'Public', 'Private nonprofit', 'Private for-profit'. Matches the enum in the Silver institution table.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 3 GLD-CSI-009 enum validity. Silver DQ rule SLV-CSI enforces the identical 3-value enum after mapping CONTROL codes 1/2/3. EDA confirmed all 68,143 non-null institution_control values in the joined dataset fall within this set (0 out-of-set). Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE and ADV-VALUE-RANGE patterns.
